top_candidates.reasoning: The engineering layer literally quotes 'kṣudraṁ hṛdaya-daurbalyaṁ tyaktvottiṣṭha' in the scenario — that is BG 2.3 verbatim. The diagnosis (petty heart-weakness) and the imperative (stand up) are the verse's signature pair. The 'small wearing the costume of large' framing maps directly to kṣudra-hṛdaya-daurbalyam in disproportionate-magnitude form.
Distant. BG 18.78 is the closing verse on Krishna-Arjuna victory. Listed only because the imperative-form is structurally adjacent. Low confidence.
BG 4.42 also closes with 'uttiṣṭha bhārata' (stand up, descendant of Bharata). Same imperative, different chapter context. Possible but the kṣudra-hṛdaya-daurbalya diagnosis is unique to 2.3.
